_meta.name: ant-fresh-flowspec-v2-2026-05-13
_meta.supersedes: ant-fresh-flowspec-spec-2026-05-13.json (v1 used non-FlowSpec generic taxonomy → render error e.data.columns.slice undefined)
_meta.purpose: Source-grounded visual map of fresh-ANT logic for JWPK to verify the system has persisted as intended.
_meta.author: @researchant
_meta.scope: Read-only mapping. Code-grounded only — routes, stores, CLI verbs, fanout queue, verifyPaneTargetState, twoCallSubmit, kind guards, persistence/auth gaps.
_meta.review_boundary: @evolveantcodex reviews for factual drift against source. NOT implementation-readiness.
_meta.schema: FlowSpec native primitives — dataPoints / components / transforms / tables / annotations.
_meta.id_convention: Stable IDs across v1 → v2 where mapping is natural (e.g. node:store:terminals → table:terminals).
_meta.counts: 6 tables, 4 dataPoints (msg-kinds), 21 components (11 routes + 10 cli-verbs), 10 transforms, 4 annotations.

annotations.label: Chat room/message/invite stores VOLATILE — wiped on every kickstart
annotations.remediation: ROOMS-PERSISTENCE-A slice — design contract on disk at docs/rooms-persistence-0-design-contract-2026-05-13.md

annotations.label: IDENTITY-GATE-POSTS BLOCKER — arbitrary authorHandle accepted, NO caller-identity validation
annotations.source: src/routes/api/chat-rooms/[roomId]/messages/+server.ts:42-46 — authorHandle = authorHandleRaw || '@you', no resolve against caller PID-tree
annotations.remediation: IDENTITY-GATE-POSTS slice (claude2 owns) — POST handler must call /api/identity/resolve internally with caller PID chain + room_id, override authorHandle from room_memberships(room_id, terminal_id)

annotations.label: Context/render contamination — UNRESOLVED security incident (real, NOT intentional test per JWPK)
annotations.source: Multi-agent observation 2026-05-13: foreign system-reminders for tool-call results not initiated by the agent (ls of v3 cli/, full Read of /Users/jamesking/.claude/skills/pptx/html2pptx.md). researchant + claude2 + evolveantclaude all observed independently. claude2 diagnostic: bleed is one-directional (results IN, posts OUT clean), automated trigger pattern, MULTI-SESSION at Claude Code session/context layer (NOT in fresh-ANT, NOT in PTY-INJECT). researchant investigation report ranked HOOK_SERVER 127.0.0.1:6457 as H1 + pptx-skill-auto-Read as H2.
annotations.remediation: Investigation ongoing. Recommended next steps: inspect HOOK_SERVER process state + logs, test by temporarily disabling pre-tool-use hooks see if bleed stops.

annotations.label: Fanout kind-guard — only human/agent messages enqueue; system/system-break excluded (recursion lockout)
annotations.source: src/lib/server/pty-inject-fanout.ts:26 (FANOUT_KINDS_ALLOWED = Set('human','agent')) + line 70 (early return on disallowed kinds)
annotations.purpose: Prevents recursion: system-emitted stale-markers don't re-fanout to all members and trigger more stale-markers

tables.label: room_memberships
tables.note: Persists across kickstart. UNIQUE(room_id, handle) — handles are room-scoped per JWPK data model.

tables.label: planModeStore (in-memory append-only event log)
tables.endpoint: process memory — wipes on kickstart, recoverable via PLAN-VISIBLE seed:overnight

components.label: POST /api/identity/resolve
components.note: Walks chain, returns handle only if room_id supplied AND room_memberships row exists

components.label: POST /api/chat-invites
components.source_ref: Admin-token gated. 503 if ANT_ADMIN_TOKEN unset, 401 if mismatch

components.label: POST /api/chat-rooms/:roomId/messages
components.note: Line 42-46: authorHandle DEFAULTS to '@you' with NO caller-identity validation. Line 67: calls fanoutMessageToRoomTerminals in try/catch (best-effort).

components.label: ant rooms post <roomId> <message>
components.note: CLI does NOT pass --handle; server defaults to '@you' or '@cli' bypassing caller-identity attribution

components.label: ant chat tail --room <roomId> [--once] [--since-ms <ts>]
components.source_ref: scripts/ant-cli-chat.mjs (tail handler) — default = indefinite polling until SIGINT

components.label: ant register --name <name> [--pane %X] [--agent-kind <kind>] [--mirror-v3]
components.source_ref: scripts/ant-cli-register.mjs:1-208 — chain walk DEFAULT-ON, --mirror-v3 opt-in only

logic.content: Walk ps -o ppid= up the tree from start_pid. Dedup via seen Set. Max depth 32. Capture pid_start via ps -o lstart= each step. Returns [{pid, pid_start}, ...]. CHAIN-WALK IS DEFAULT-ON in fresh-ANT (vs v3 --chain opt-in).
Reject system/system-break (line 70). Look up room_memberships. Filter sender. For each member with tmux_target_pane: enqueue via queueKeyFor(roomId, terminalId). kind ∈ {human,agent} only.
Per-(roomId,terminalId) compound key. If queue empty, schedule 500ms flush via setTimeout. Subsequent items append to same batch. On flush: invoke callback with all queued items.

Stage 1: runScrubbedTmux('capture-pane', '-t', pane, '-p', '-S', '-50'). If error → stale. If success+empty → unknown. If success+non-empty → Stage 2. Stage 2: matchReadyStateFor — for claude_code: captured.includes('│ >' OR '❯') AND NOT captured.includes('esc to interrupt').
agent_kind=claude_code: '│ >' OR '❯' marker present AND NOT 'esc to interrupt'. agent_kind ∈ {codex,aider,gemini}: NOT YET IMPLEMENTED, returns false. agent_kind null: false.

1. pasteBufferToTarget(pane, text). 2. scheduler 150ms → sendEnterToTarget(pane) wrapped in try/catch → onScheduledFailure on InjectTmuxFailure. 3. If agent_kind=claude_code: scheduler 300ms → SECOND sendEnter (Claude Code TUI requires double-return per v3 ask-pty-bridge.ts source).
load-buffer (status check + throw on non-zero) → paste-buffer (status check, defers throw so cleanup runs) → delete-buffer (status check). Each via runScrubbedTmux.
runScrubbedTmux('send-keys', '-t', pane, 'Enter'). spawnSync status === 0 assertion; throws InjectTmuxFailure on non-zero.

spawnSync from node:child_process. Deletes TMUX, TMUX_PANE, TMUX_PLUGIN_MANAGER_PATH from childEnv. spawn(TMUX_BIN, args, { input, env }) — argv arrays only, NO shell interpolation. Single entry-point for ALL tmux calls (B3 strict).

Module-level Map<roomId+handle, lastTs>. If now - lastTs >= 60min OR no entry: postSystemMessage(roomId, '@<handle> appears offline (pane stale)'). Else silent-drop. Recursion lockout via msg-kind=system being excluded from fanout.

_handoff_notes_for_evolveantclaude: v2 spec: re-authored to FlowSpec NATIVE PRIMITIVES (dataPoints / components / transforms / tables) per JWPK Do-it-properly directive after v1 render error e.data.columns.slice undefined.
Use mcp__flowspec__create_node with the appropriate FlowSpec type for each entity: tables → Table, dataPoints → DataPoint, components → Component, transforms → Transform.
Tables now have proper `columns` arrays — should resolve the .slice undefined error. Other primitives don't need columns.

Edges in FlowSpec native are typically derived from displays/captures/inputs/outputs relationships, not declared separately. The renderer should be able to infer the dataflow from these fields. If explicit edges are still needed, they can be derived: component.captures[X] ← upstream-of-X, component.displays[X] ← downstream-of-X, transform.inputs[X] ← upstream, transform.outputs[X] ← downstream.
Annotations array preserved separately — render as visual flags.
Counts: 6 tables, 4 dataPoints, 21 components (11 routes + 10 cli-verbs), 10 transforms, 4 annotations.
Recommend: clear the existing canvas in project 31a7ba82 OR create a fresh project ant-fresh-flowspec-v2; the v1 generic-taxonomy nodes are incompatible with v2 native taxonomy.
v1 stable IDs preserved where mapping is natural (table:terminals was node:store:terminals etc.) so factual-drift review can grep both.
evolveantcodex factual-drift review: same source_refs as v1 — files unchanged.
